key_insight: INCARBench shows that LLMs can often produce plausible VASP INCAR settings, but scientifically correct configurations remain much harder, especially when multiple physics constraints interact.
engineering_relevance: For computational materials workflows, this means LLMs may assist with input setup, but their outputs still need expert validation before running expensive first-principles calculations.
method: Benchmark for VASP INCAR configuration generation
Benchmark for INCAR repair of incorrect settings
Evaluated 19 model configurations across semantic, policy, and task-critical correctness
evidence_signal: Across 19 model configurations, several models scored well on semantic and policy accuracy, but task-critical correctness was much lower, especially for DFT+U, magnetism, and correlated-material settings.
watch_item: A key concern is that preserving already-correct configuration values during repair remains difficult, so automated edits can inadvertently break valid physics settings.
paper_title: INCARBench: A Benchmark for Scientific Configuration in VASP INCAR by Large Language Models
